behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is low: this is primarily a deterministic key-generation boundary (canonicalization + hashing/encoding) with no required I/O. REAL-INFRA and LLM-DEP do not apply. A focused in-process test can catch real failures like order-dependent serialization, type normalization bugs, and unstable hashing.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the real key-builder function with table-driven and property-style cases: same params in different map/key orders produce identical keys; semantically different params produce different keys; edge types (null, booleans, arrays, unicode, numeric strings vs numbers) do not accidentally collapse. Run determinism checks across repeated calls in one process.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is meaningful if TTL correctness depends on config wiring/parsing (category mapping, duration units) rather than only arithmetic. A pure unit test of a mocked config object can miss real failures in config interpretation. REAL-INFRA is not required beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test TTL computation by loading the real test configuration source used in app wiring (same parser/mapper), then requesting TTL for categories (e.g., user_profiles=5m, product_catalog=1h) and asserting exact durations and fallback behavior for unknown categories.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA can be essential if strategy switching uses real process/OS memory signals (RSS/cgroup/procfs). MOCK-HIDE risk is high: mocking memory pressure can always pass while real telemetry or threshold units behave differently. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: System-test a running cache process with a very low threshold; verify active policy starts as LRU, then allocate memory in-process (or via workload) until actual measured usage crosses threshold, and assert the policy flips to LFU via exposed metrics/state endpoint. Include hysteresis/edge checks around the threshold boundary.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is high for event/wiring bugs between DB write path and cache invalidation path. Per LEVEL-DEFS, local DB usage fits Integration. System is less necessary unless remote infra/network behavior is the target.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test with a real local PostgreSQL instance and real cache components: seed rows and corresponding cached keys, perform INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on target table, trigger/consume invalidation path, then assert all and only matching cache keys are removed while unrelated keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries (LRU under LRU, LFU under LFU) when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Although core ranking logic can be unit-tested, real failures often occur in module interaction: access tracking, frequency counters, and store capacity enforcement. MOCK-HIDE risk exists if policy internals are mocked. No OS/network/LLM dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test the real cache store plus policy module: fill cache to capacity, perform controlled access patterns, insert one additional entry, and assert exact evicted key under LRU and LFU modes respectively. Include tie-breaker expectations and counter update timing after reads/writes.
